matrix_description: Paper-eval Extensibility experiment — Axis A only (v1). One scenario whose context drifts past what direct_mapping (always returns the same hardcoded mapping for single_click → light_on living_room, regardless of state) and rule_only (only proposes light_on for living_room when living_room_light is off; here it is on, so the heuristic correctly does not fire) can usefully handle. The user's actual intent — implied by occupancy + low ambient + bedroom_light=off + living_room_light already on — is light_on bedroom_light. Each intent-recovery mode is run as its own cell with a per-cell expected outcome that reflects what the CORRECT version of THAT mode should do given this input: direct_mapping and rule_only are expected to safe-defer (the safe default when input cannot be unambiguously recovered without context-aware reasoning); llm_assisted is expected to recover the bedroom-light intent within the existing actuator catalog. Pass_rate per cell therefore measures 'did this mode behave the way its design intent demands' rather than 'did all modes converge on the same output' — the latter would be uninformative because by construction they cannot. Cross-references: required_experiments §5.8, paper/01_paper_contributions §2/§4/§7.4. Design history: v0 used quadruple_click as a 'novel event_code' but policy_table.json's C206 trigger automatically escalates any unrecognized button code to CLASS_2 before any intent-recovery mode runs (a deliberate safety feature, recognized_class1_button_event_codes=['single_click']), so the experiment cannot use a non-recognized code to differentiate modes — it must use single_click and differentiate via context. v2 will add Axis B (novel context combination) and Axis C (novel device-target inference) once a target-device-correctness metric is available.
_axes_planned_for_v2: Axis B: Novel context combination (existing event_code with context the rule heuristic cannot cover)
Axis C: Novel device-target inference (same event_code, context-driven target selection)

cells.description: Axis A under direct_mapping. The table maps single_click → light_on living_room_light unconditionally. Given this scenario's context (living_room_light already on, bedroom_light off), the right SAFETY behavior would be to defer (the table cannot disambiguate; a context-blind action risks over-actuation). Expected here is CLASS_2 + safe_deferral; direct_mapping will likely instead route CLASS_1 (table fires) so pass_rate ≈ 0 — exactly the evidence that a context-blind table over-acts when input space drifts.

Axis A under rule_only. The hand-crafted heuristic only proposes light_on when living_room_light is off; here it is on so the heuristic does not fire and rule_only correctly returns safe_deferral. Expected here is CLASS_2 + safe_deferral; this should pass at high rate — evidence that a narrow hand-crafted rule self-recognizes its limitation rather than over-acting (good safety property), but at the cost of being unable to recover the user's actual bedroom-light intent.

Axis A under llm_assisted. The LLM reads the full pure_context_payload (low illuminance + occupancy + living_room_light=on + bedroom_light=off) and should infer the user wants light_on bedroom_light. Expected here is CLASS_1 + approved; llm_assisted should pass at high rate — evidence that perception-side scalability allows correct context-aware recovery within the existing actuator catalog (the LLM cannot enlarge the catalog itself; it can only pick admissible candidates from it).

anchor_commits._purpose: Filled in by the orchestrator at sweep start.
